description: Customer Order Analytics Pipeline reads from Oracle source tables (sales.orders and crm.customers) and a CSV flat file (order_items.csv), performs currency conversion lookups to standardize amounts to USD, enriches product data with hierarchy and margin information, filters for valid orders and items, joins orders with customer demographics and order line items, aggregates metrics by customer (revenue, order count, product diversity), assigns customer tier based on revenue and order thresholds, calculates engagement scores, deduplicates by customer, and writes the final customer analytics summary to PostgreSQL analytics table and a CSV export file.

columns.name: total_amount
columns.description: Total order amount before discount and tax in original currency

columns.name: discount_amount
columns.description: Discount applied to the order in original currency

columns.name: tax_amount
columns.description: Tax amount for the order in original currency

columns.name: payment_method
columns.description: Payment method used (e.g., credit card, PayPal)

columns.name: order_status
columns.description: Current status of the order (confirmed, shipped, delivered, etc.)

columns.name: currency
columns.description: ISO currency code (USD, EUR, GBP, etc.)

columns.name: store_id
columns.description: Store identifier if applicable, nullable for online orders

columns.name: is_active
columns.description: Flag indicating if customer is active (1=active, 0=inactive)

columns.name: item_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for each order line item

columns.name: line_total
columns.description: Total for this line item (quantity * unit_price)

columns.name: is_private_label
columns.description: Flag indicating if product is private label

columns.name: total_orders
columns.description: Count of distinct orders for customer

columns.name: gross_revenue
columns.description: Total revenue in USD before discount

columns.name: net_revenue
columns.description: Net revenue in USD (total - discount + tax)

columns.name: customer_tier
columns.description: Customer tier: Platinum (≥$50K & ≥50 orders), Gold (≥$20K & ≥20), Silver (≥$5K & ≥5), Bronze (all others)

columns.name: engagement_score
columns.description: Composite engagement score based on order frequency, product diversity, tenure, and margin

columns.name: last_order_date
columns.description: Date of customer's most recent order

components.name: read_orders
components.description: Reads orders from Oracle sales.orders table. Filters to orders placed on the specified DATA_DATE and excludes cancelled and fraudulent orders. This is the primary source for order-level transaction data.
components.sql_text: SELECT order_id, customer_id, order_date, total_amount, discount_amount, tax_amount, shipping_cost, payment_method, order_status, channel, currency, store_id FROM sales.orders WHERE order_date = TO_DATE('${DATA_DATE}', 'YYYYMMDD') AND order_status NOT IN ('cancelled', 'fraudulent')

components.name: read_customers
components.description: Reads customer master data from Oracle crm.customers table. Filters to active customers only (is_active = 1). Provides customer demographics and segmentation information.
components.sql_text: SELECT customer_id, first_name, last_name, email, signup_date, country, region, segment, is_active FROM crm.customers WHERE is_active = 1

components.name: read_order_items
components.description: Reads order line items from CSV flat file. Each record represents a product within an order with quantity and pricing information.

components.name: lookup_exchange_rates
components.description: Enriches orders with currency exchange rates. Looks up the USD exchange rate for each order's currency and calculates USD-equivalent amounts for total, discount, tax, and net amounts. This standardizes all monetary values to USD for consistent reporting.
components.sql_text: Lookup exchange_rates.dat on currency_code; out.amount_usd = in.total_amount * lookup(in.currency).usd_rate; out.discount_usd = in.discount_amount * lookup(in.currency).usd_rate; out.tax_usd = in.tax_amount * lookup(in.currency).usd_rate; out.net_amount_usd = (in.total_amount - in.discount_amount + in.tax_amount) * lookup(in.currency).usd_rate

components.name: lookup_product_hierarchy
components.description: Enriches order items with product hierarchy and margin data. Looks up department, sub-category, brand, private label flag, and margin percentage for each product. Calculates estimated margin in USD for each line item.
components.sql_text: Lookup product_hierarchy.dat on product_id; out.estimated_margin = in.line_total * lookup(in.product_id).margin_pct / 100

components.name: filter_valid_orders
components.description: Filters orders to include only valid transactions: amount_usd > 0 (positive value), order_status in confirmed/shipped/delivered (completed orders), and customer_id is not null. Rejects orders that fail these criteria.
components.sql_text: WHERE in.amount_usd > 0 AND in.order_status IN ('confirmed', 'shipped', 'delivered') AND in.customer_id IS NOT NULL

components.name: filter_high_value_items
components.description: Filters order items to include only valid line items: quantity > 0 and unit_price > 0. Removes zero-quantity or zero-price items.

components.name: join_order_customer
components.description: Inner join of filtered orders with customer master data on customer_id. Enriches each order with customer demographics (name, email, country, region, segment) and calculates days since customer signup. This creates an order-customer view.
components.sql_text: INNER JOIN on customer_id; out.customer_name = string_concat(right.first_name, ' ', right.last_name); out.days_since_signup = date_difference(right.signup_date, left.order_date, 'days')

components.name: join_order_items
components.description: Left outer join of order-customer view with filtered order items on order_id. Preserves all orders even if they have no line items. Combines order-level metrics with product-level details (product name, category, department, brand, quantity, pricing, margin).
components.sql_text: LEFT OUTER JOIN on order_id; all left columns passed through, right columns (product details) included

components.name: rollup_by_customer
components.description: Aggregates order and item data by customer_id. Calculates key customer metrics: total_orders (distinct count), total_items (sum of quantities), gross_revenue, discounts, taxes, net_revenue (all in USD), average/max order values, distinct product count, top category/brand (mode), total margin, margin percentage, first/last order dates, and days since signup. Groups all transactions for each customer into a single summary row.
components.sql_text: GROUP BY customer_id; out.total_orders = count_distinct(in.order_id); out.total_items = sum(in.quantity); out.gross_revenue = sum(in.amount_usd); out.total_discount = sum(in.discount_usd); out.total_tax = sum(in.tax_usd); out.net_revenue = sum(in.net_amount_usd); out.avg_order_value = mean(in.amount_usd); out.max_order_value = max(in.amount_usd); out.distinct_products = count_distinct(in.product_id); out.top_category = mode(in.category); out.top_brand = mode(in.brand); out.total_margin = sum(in.estimated_margin); out.margin_pct = sum(in.estimated_margin) / if_null(sum(in.amount_usd), 1) * 100; out.first_order_date = min(in.order_date); out.last_order_date = max(in.order_date); out.days_since_signup = first(in.days_since_signup)

components.name: assign_customer_tier
components.description: Assigns customer tier and engagement score based on aggregated metrics. Tier assignment: Platinum (net_revenue >= $50K AND total_orders >= 50), Gold (net_revenue >= $20K AND total_orders >= 20), Silver (net_revenue >= $5K AND total_orders >= 5), Bronze (all others). Engagement score is composite: 30/20/10 points for order frequency (>=20/>=10/else), 20/10/5 for product diversity (>=15/>=5/else), 25/15/10 for tenure (>=365/>=180/else days), 25/15/5 for margin (>=30%/>=15%/else). Adds ETL run date.
components.sql_text: out.customer_tier = IF in.net_revenue >= 50000 AND in.total_orders >= 50 THEN 'Platinum' ELSE IF in.net_revenue >= 20000 AND in.total_orders >= 20 THEN 'Gold' ELSE IF in.net_revenue >= 5000 AND in.total_orders >= 5 THEN 'Silver' ELSE 'Bronze'; out.engagement_score = (IF in.total_orders >= 20 THEN 30 ELSE IF in.total_orders >= 10 THEN 20 ELSE 10) + (IF in.distinct_products >= 15 THEN 20 ELSE IF in.distinct_products >= 5 THEN 10 ELSE 5) + (IF in.days_since_signup >= 365 THEN 25 ELSE IF in.days_since_signup >= 180 THEN 15 ELSE 10) + (IF in.margin_pct >= 30 THEN 25 ELSE IF in.margin_pct >= 15 THEN 15 ELSE 5); out.etl_date = today()

components.name: dedup_customers
components.description: Deduplicates customer records by customer_id, keeping the first occurrence. Ensures one row per customer in the output. Separates unique records from duplicates.

components.name: partition_output
components.description: Partitions deduplicated customer records using round-robin distribution across 4 partitions (controlled by $PARALLELISM parameter). Enables parallel writing to target systems.

components.name: write_customer_analytics
components.description: Writes customer analytics summary to PostgreSQL analytics.customer_daily_summary table using merge mode on (customer_id, etl_date). This allows incremental updates of customer metrics by date. Target table contains all customer metrics, tier, and engagement score.

components.name: write_summary_file
components.description: Writes a subset of customer analytics to CSV file for distribution. Includes customer_id, name, region, tier, order count, net revenue, engagement score, and ETL date. Provides a simplified summary for business users.
components.sql_text: Write to /data/output/${DATA_DATE}/customer_summary_${DATA_DATE}.csv as delimited CSV with header

column_lineage.notes: Primary key for customer analytics; flows unchanged through entire pipeline
Concatenates first and last name from customer master; aggregated using first() function in rollup
Customer email from CRM; aggregated using first() function
Customer country from CRM; aggregated using first() function
Customer region from CRM; aggregated using first() function
Customer segment classification from CRM; aggregated using first() function
Count of distinct orders per customer; used in tier assignment logic (Platinum >=50, Gold >=20, Silver >=5)
Total quantity of items ordered across all orders
Total order amount in USD before discount; converted from original currency using exchange rate lookup
Total discount amount in USD; converted from original currency
Total tax amount in USD; converted from original currency
Net revenue in USD (total - discount + tax); key metric for tier assignment (Platinum >=50000, Gold >=20000, Silver >=5000)
Average order value in USD across all customer orders
Maximum order value in USD for the customer
Count of distinct products purchased; used in engagement score (>=15 pts=20, >=5 pts=10, else 5)
Most frequently purchased product category
Most frequently purchased brand
Total estimated margin in USD across all customer purchases
Margin percentage (total_margin / gross_revenue * 100); used in engagement score (>=30% pts=25, >=15% pts=15, else 5)
Date of customer's first order
Date of customer's most recent order

column_lineage.transformation: IF net_revenue >= 50000 AND total_orders >= 50 THEN 'Platinum' ELSE IF net_revenue >= 20000 AND total_orders >= 20 THEN 'Gold' ELSE IF net_revenue >= 5000 AND total_orders >= 5 THEN 'Silver' ELSE 'Bronze'

intermediate_steps.expression: IF in.net_revenue >= 50000 AND in.total_orders >= 50 THEN 'Platinum' ELSE IF in.net_revenue >= 20000 AND in.total_orders >= 20 THEN 'Gold' ELSE IF in.net_revenue >= 5000 AND in.total_orders >= 5 THEN 'Silver' ELSE 'Bronze'

column_lineage.notes: Customer tier assignment based on net revenue and order count thresholds: Platinum (>=50K & >=50 orders), Gold (>=20K & >=20), Silver (>=5K & >=5), Bronze (all others)

column_lineage.transformation: (IF total_orders >= 20 THEN 30 ELSE IF total_orders >= 10 THEN 20 ELSE 10) + (IF distinct_products >= 15 THEN 20 ELSE IF distinct_products >= 5 THEN 10 ELSE 5) + (IF days_since_signup >= 365 THEN 25 ELSE IF days_since_signup >= 180 THEN 15 ELSE 10) + (IF margin_pct >= 30 THEN 25 ELSE IF margin_pct >= 15 THEN 15 ELSE 5)

intermediate_steps.expression: (IF in.total_orders >= 20 THEN 30 ELSE IF in.total_orders >= 10 THEN 20 ELSE 10) + (IF in.distinct_products >= 15 THEN 20 ELSE IF in.distinct_products >= 5 THEN 10 ELSE 5) + (IF in.days_since_signup >= 365 THEN 25 ELSE IF in.days_since_signup >= 180 THEN 15 ELSE 10) + (IF in.margin_pct >= 30 THEN 25 ELSE IF in.margin_pct >= 15 THEN 15 ELSE 5)

column_lineage.notes: Composite engagement score (0-100 scale): Order frequency (30/20/10 pts for >=20/>=10/else), Product diversity (20/10/5 pts for >=15/>=5/else), Tenure (25/15/10 pts for >=365/>=180/else days), Margin contribution (25/15/5 pts for >=30%/>=15%/else)
ETL run date; used as part of merge key in target table
Customer ID for CSV export
Full customer name for CSV export
Customer region for CSV export

column_lineage.notes: Customer tier for CSV export
Order count for CSV export
Net revenue in USD for CSV export

column_lineage.transformation: (IF total_orders >= 20 THEN 30 ELSE IF total_orders >= 10 THEN 20 ELSE 10) + (IF distinct_products >= 15 THEN 20 ELSE IF distinct_products >= 5 THEN 10 ELSE 5) + (IF days_since_signup >= 365 THEN 25 ELSE IF days_since_signup >= 180 THEN 15 ELSE 10) + (IF margin_pct >= 30 THEN 25 ELSE IF margin_pct >= 15 THEN 15 ELSE 5)
column_lineage.notes: Engagement score for CSV export
ETL run date for CSV export

data_flow_edges.edge_label: read from Oracle; filter: order_date = ${DATA_DATE} AND order_status NOT IN ('cancelled', 'fraudulent')
lookup exchange_rates.dat on currency_code; enrich with usd_rate and calculate USD amounts
lookup product_hierarchy.dat on product_id; enrich with department, brand, margin
filter: amount_usd > 0 AND order_status IN ('confirmed', 'shipped', 'delivered') AND customer_id IS NOT NULL
left side of inner join on customer_id
right side of inner join on customer_id; enrich orders with customer demographics
left side of left outer join on order_id
right side of left outer join on order_id; add product details to orders
aggregate by customer_id; calculate metrics: total_orders, total_items, revenue, discount, tax, margin, product diversity
assign tier and engagement score based on revenue/order thresholds and engagement metrics
partition by round-robin into 4 partitions for parallel write
write subset to CSV file for distribution
